patient_information.patient_address: 45 E Parkway Ave, New York, NY 21022

physician_contact.office_address: 36 E Ridgeview St, New York, NY 21023

clinical_information.diagnosis: Moderate to severe atopic dermatitis (persistent atopic dermatitis) in an 8-year-old female
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Topical triamcinolone cream and pimecrolimus up to twice daily plus regular emollients (Aquaphor) used for approximately 5 weeks without significant relief; symptoms persist (pruritic dry patches, excoriation, occasional bleeding).
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: SCORAD 35 (moderate to severe) with >10% body surface area affected. Pruritic, erythematous excoriated plaques on flexural areas (elbows, knees) and face causing disturbed sleep and occasional skin bleeding from scratching.
clinical_information.prognosis_and_risk_if_not_approved: Without approval, the patient is at risk for persistent moderate-to-severe disease with ongoing sleep disruption, continued skin breakdown and excoriation with potential for secondary skin infection, impaired quality of life, and progression of symptoms due to inadequate control with topical therapy.
clinical_information.clinical_rationale_for_urgency: Patient has moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (SCORAD 35, >10% BSA) that has failed an adequate trial (~5 weeks) of high-potency topical steroid and topical calcineurin inhibitor plus emollients. Systemic therapy (dupilumab) is recommended now to achieve better symptom control and prevent ongoing morbidity (sleep loss, bleeding/excoriation, risk of infection).

treatment_request.dosage: 300 mg initial dose (two 150 mg injections), then 300 mg every 2 weeks
treatment_request.rationale: Severe atopic dermatitis; inadequate response to triamcinolone 0.5% ointment (5 weeks) and pimecrolimus 1% cream (5 weeks).

E. Prurigo Nodularis  
 
1.  Initial Authorization 
 
a.   Dupixent will be approved based on all of  the following criteria: 
 
(1)  Diagnosis of prurigo nodularis 
 
-AND- 
 (2)  Patient has greater than or equal to 20 nodular lesions 
 
-AND- 
 (3)  History of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to previous prurigo nodularis 
treatment (s) (e.g., topical corticosteroids, topical calcineurin inhibitor s, topical 
capsaicin)   
 
-AND- 
 
(4)  Patient is not receiving Dupixent in combination with either of the following:  
 
(a) Biologic immunomodulator [e.g., Adbry (tralokinumab- ldrm)]  
(b) Janus kinase inhibitor [e.g., Rinvoq (upadacitinib), Xeljanz/XR (tofacitinib), 
Opzelura (topical ruxolitinib), Cibinqo (abrocitinib)]  
 
-AND- 
 
(5)  Prescribed by one of the following:  
 
(a) Dermatologist  
(b) Allergist  
(c) Immunologist  
 
Authorization will be issued for 6 months.

a.

decision.reason: The documentation confirms a diagnosis of moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis and failure of two topical therapy classes (topical corticosteroid: triamcinolone; topical calcineurin inhibitor: pimecrolimus). However, the policy requires documentation that the patient is NOT receiving Dupixent in combination with specified agents (e.g., other biologic immunomodulators or JAK inhibitors) and documentation of the topical therapy trials (drug names and dates of trial and/or documented contraindications). The submitted case does not explicitly state that the patient is not receiving Dupixent in combination with other biologic or JAK inhibitor therapies, nor does it provide exact dates for the topical trials. Per policy, these items must be documented to determine approval.
